name: carousel
description: A responsive, accessible carousel — a scroll-snap viewport of slides with prev/next controls and indicator dots (WAI-ARIA Carousel pattern). Adapts to its container by default.

modifiers.name: peek
modifiers.description: Reveal a sliver of the adjacent slides on either side of the active one. Works in both orientations (inline edges when horizontal, block edges when vertical).

options.name: none
options.description: No peek — the active slide fills the viewport (the default).

options.name: sm
options.description: A small peek.

options.name: md
options.description: A medium peek.

options.name: lg
options.description: A large peek.

modifiers.name: gap
modifiers.description: The spacing between slides. A t-shirt scale re-pointing --primitiv-carousel-gap to a spacing token; `md` is the default. Works in both orientations (the gap runs on the scroll axis) and composes with every other variant. Note: the `padding` modifier couples the gap to its inset for a clean framed track, so it overrides this within a padded track.

options.name: none
options.description: No gap — the slides sit flush.

options.name: sm
options.description: A small gap.

options.name: md
options.description: A medium gap (the default).

options.name: lg
options.description: A large gap.

modifiers.name: padding
modifiers.description: Make the viewport a padded, framed track: pad the slides inward from the viewport edges and draw the track outline (border + rounded corners). The gap is coupled to the padding so the resting track doesn't itself reveal a neighbour; add `peek` for a deliberate reveal within the track. The background fill is opt-in via the `surface` modifier — padding alone renders an outlined track.

options.name: none
options.description: No viewport padding — a bare, frameless scroll box (the default).

options.name: sm
options.description: A small inset.

options.name: md
options.description: A medium inset.

options.name: lg
options.description: A large inset.

modifiers.name: surface
modifiers.description: Opt into the viewport track's background fill. Off by default (a framed track is just an outline); pairs with `padding` to render a filled, framed track.

options.name: none
options.description: No fill — the track is transparent (the default).

options.name: subtle
options.description: Fill the track with the subtle surface token.

modifiers.name: placement
modifiers.description: Where the controls sit relative to the imagery — the one axis that is off-vs-on the slide. `external` (the default) keeps the controls off the imagery, in the space around the viewport. `overlay` insets them on the slide for edge-to-edge imagery. How the controls are *arranged* (one bar vs prev/next flanking the edges) is the orthogonal `cluster` axis; `side` / `distribution` / `align` / `orientation` then compose on top of both.

options.name: external
options.description: Controls off the imagery, in the space around the viewport (the default). A CSS grid places the parts in tracks beside the viewport.

options.name: overlay
options.description: Controls on the imagery — absolutely positioned over the slide on a translucent scrim, for edge-to-edge photography.

modifiers.name: side
modifiers.description: Which cross-axis edge the indicator cluster (or the joined bar) sits on, relative to the scroll direction. `after` is the trailing edge (below the viewport when horizontal, the inline-end/right side when vertical); `before` is the leading edge (above / inline-start). Orientation-relative and RTL-safe — it composes with `orientation` to reach all four physical edges. Read by both placements and both clusters.

options.name: after
options.description: Trailing edge — below the viewport (horizontal) or the end/right side (vertical). The default.

options.name: before
options.description: Leading edge — above the viewport (horizontal) or the start/left side (vertical).

modifiers.name: distribution
modifiers.description: How the controls spread along their edge. For `joined` it drives the whole prev/indicators/next bar — `group` (the default) bunches them, `stretch` pushes prev/next to the extremes with the indicators centred (space-between). For `split` it governs the indicator cluster (prev/next stay flanking the viewport edges) — `group` bunches the dots, `stretch` spreads them across the full edge. Applies to both placements.

options.name: group
options.description: Bunch the controls together with a fixed gap, positioned by `align` (the default).

options.name: stretch
options.description: Spread the controls across the full edge (space-between).

modifiers.name: align
modifiers.description: Where the grouped controls sit along their edge (only read under `distribution=group`; `stretch` fills the edge, so alignment is moot). `center` is the default; `start` / `end` pin the cluster to the leading / trailing end — the whole bar for `joined`, the indicator cluster for `split`. Applies to both placements. Logical, so it mirrors under RTL and follows the scroll axis when vertical.

options.name: start
options.description: Pin the control cluster to the start of the edge (left / top, mirrored under RTL).

options.name: center
options.description: Centre the control cluster on the edge (the default).

options.name: end
options.description: Pin the control cluster to the end of the edge (right / bottom, mirrored under RTL).

modifiers.name: cluster
modifiers.description: How the controls are arranged — the orthogonal companion to `placement`, read by both `external` and `overlay`. `split` (the default) sends prev/next to the viewport's two scroll-axis edges (flanking it — left/right when horizontal, top/bottom when vertical) and leaves the indicators as a separate cluster positioned by `side` / `distribution` / `align`. `joined` bundles prev + indicators + next into one `<CarouselControls>` bar that travels together, positioned as a unit by `side` / `distribution` / `align`. Compose the parts inside `<CarouselControls>` for `joined`, as direct children of the root for `split`. Together with `placement` this is the full 2×2 — external/overlay × split/joined.

options.name: split
options.description: Prev/next flank the viewport's scroll-axis edges; the indicators are a separate cluster (the default). Compose the parts as direct children of the root.

options.name: joined
options.description: Bundle prev + indicators + next into one bar that moves together; compose them inside `<CarouselControls>`.

modifiers.name: indicators
modifiers.description: What the indicators look like. `dots` (the default) is the compact dot row; `thumbnails` swaps each indicator for a rounded-rect image thumbnail — the active one ringed in the primary colour, the classic gallery pattern. Supply the thumbnail content as children of each `` (an `` or a background element).

options.name: dots
options.description: Compact dots — one per page (the default).

options.name: thumbnails
options.description: Image thumbnails — each indicator shows its slide's thumbnail, the active one ringed in the primary colour.

options.name: md
options.description: Medium rounded corners (the default).

options.name: none
options.description: Sharp, un-rounded corners.

modifiers.name: ratio
modifiers.description: The slide's aspect ratio (read in the horizontal orientation — the vertical viewport owns its own ratio via --primitiv-carousel-vertical-aspect-ratio). Re-points --primitiv-carousel-slide-aspect-ratio to a common media ratio; override the knob directly for a bespoke value.

options.name: square
options.description: 1:1 — a square slide.

options.name: standard
options.description: 4:3 — the classic photo ratio.

options.name: wide
options.description: 16:9 — widescreen (the default).

options.name: ultrawide
options.description: 21:9 — cinematic ultra-wide.
